rationales.gemini: The algorithm failed to segregate noise properly. Many samples (like 1, 2, 7, and 8) are generic introductory queries that clearly belong in Cluster 0 or 6. Only 'fault tolerance' is a true outlier.
rationales.claude: Good noise segregation overall, though 3-4 questions show some relatedness around general Hadoop understanding/benefits. Most questions are genuinely diverse (fault tolerance, background, everything, distributed processing). Label is mostly appropriate but the cluster could have been split better.
rationales.glm: The noise pile hides a group of related questions seeking comprehensive general understanding (definition, benefits, purpose). Only questions 4 (vague) and 6 (specific fault tolerance concept) are truly singular. The algorithm underperformed by not grouping the thematically related questions together.

chain_of_thought.core_theme_analysis: Questions meant to be unique or outliers that don't fit into other clusters.
chain_of_thought.outlier_check: Several questions here are NOT rare (e.g., 'pengertian, kelebihan & kekurangan' or 'tujuan dan manfaat') and should have been grouped into Cluster 0, 4, or 10.
chain_of_thought.label_assessment: The label is technically accurate for a noise pile, but the algorithm failed by putting standard questions here.

gemini.rationale: The algorithm failed to segregate noise properly. Many samples (like 1, 2, 7, and 8) are generic introductory queries that clearly belong in Cluster 0 or 6. Only 'fault tolerance' is a true outlier.

chain_of_thought.core_theme_analysis: This is the noise/outlier cluster, so questions should be diverse and not form coherent subgroups.
chain_of_thought.outlier_check: Questions cover disparate topics: background/history (Q1), role in big data (Q2), distributed processing (Q3), generic 'everything needed' (Q4), purpose/benefits (Q5), fault tolerance definition (Q6), pros/cons (Q7), detailed definition (Q8). However, Q2, Q5, Q7, and Q8 could arguably form a subgroup about 'general understanding of Hadoop benefits and characteristics'.
chain_of_thought.label_assessment: The label 'Rare/Singular Topics' is appropriate for a noise cluster, though some questions (Q2, Q5, Q7, Q8) show moderate relatedness around general Hadoop understanding.

claude.rationale: Good noise segregation overall, though 3-4 questions show some relatedness around general Hadoop understanding/benefits. Most questions are genuinely diverse (fault tolerance, background, everything, distributed processing). Label is mostly appropriate but the cluster could have been split better.

chain_of_thought.core_theme_analysis: Questions vary from comprehensive understanding (1, 2, 5, 7, 8) to specific concepts (6 - fault tolerance) to vague statements (4 - 'semua yang diperlukan').
chain_of_thought.outlier_check: Hidden thematic group detected: questions 1, 2, 5, 7, and 8 all share a common theme of seeking comprehensive general understanding (definition, benefits, purpose) and could have formed a coherent cluster.
chain_of_thought.label_assessment: The 'Rare/Singular Topics' label is not fully appropriate because approximately 5-6 questions share a thematic overlap around comprehensive understanding, meaning they're not truly singular/outlier questions.

glm.rationale: The noise pile hides a group of related questions seeking comprehensive general understanding (definition, benefits, purpose). Only questions 4 (vague) and 6 (specific fault tolerance concept) are truly singular. The algorithm underperformed by not grouping the thematically related questions together.

fleiss_kappa.note: Fleiss' κ measures agreement beyond chance. Susceptible to the 'Kappa Paradox' if most ratings are perfect.

gwet_ac2.note: Gwet's AC2 (2008) with Quadratic Weights. Resolves the Kappa Paradox for ordinal data. Interpreted via Altman's (1991) guidelines.
